630adb572eacc381ae57fdb7.question: This figure once had a nightmare where she used the blood of a murdered man to put out an inferno in her house. Nonnus calls her the mother of Faunus by Poseidon. Picus’s rejection of this woman resulted in him being changed into a woodpecker, and following Glaucus’s rejection of her, she sprinkled special herbs in bathwater to turn Scylla into a monster. This inhabitant of Aiaia purified her niece of murder and was thwarted with the moly plant, which Hermes gave to Odysseus. For 10 points, identify Medea’s sorceress aunt who changed Odysseus’s men into swine.
630adb572eacc381ae57fdb7.question_sanitized: This figure once had a nightmare where she used the blood of a murdered man to put out an inferno in her house. Nonnus calls her the mother of Faunus by Poseidon. Picus's rejection of this woman resulted in him being changed into a woodpecker, and following Glaucus's rejection of her, she sprinkled special herbs in bathwater to turn Scylla into a monster. This inhabitant of Aiaia purified her niece of murder and was thwarted with the moly plant, which Hermes gave to Odysseus. For 10 points, identify Medea's sorceress aunt who changed Odysseus's men into swine.

630adb572eacc381ae57fdc3.question: An object guarded by one of these figures is also protected by Serket. These figures were among the seven guardians of Osiris’s body, and they also formed the bowl of the Big Dipper. These gods were described as pillars of Shu, and were each associated with one of the cardinal directions. One of these deities, Duamutef, claims that he comes to rescue Osiris from an assailant, and another, Imsety, has the role of lifting up corpses. For 10 points, name quartet of gods who guarded the Canopic Jars, who were said to be children of the son of Osiris and Isis.
630adb572eacc381ae57fdc3.question_sanitized: An object guarded by one of these figures is also protected by Serket. These figures were among the seven guardians of Osiris's body, and they also formed the bowl of the Big Dipper. These gods were described as pillars of Shu, and were each associated with one of the cardinal directions. One of these deities, Duamutef, claims that he comes to rescue Osiris from an assailant, and another, Imsety, has the role of lifting up corpses. For 10 points, name quartet of gods who guarded the Canopic Jars, who were said to be children of the son of Osiris and Isis.
630adb572eacc381ae57fdc3.answer: sons of Horus [Accept guardians of the Canopic Jars before mentioned or Hapi, Imsety, Duamutef, and Qebuesenuef in any order before Duamutef is mentioned]
630adb572eacc381ae57fdc3.answer_sanitized: sons of Horus [Accept guardians of the Canopic Jars before mentioned or Hapi, Imsety, Duamutef, and Qebuesenuef in any order before Duamutef is mentioned]

630adb572eacc381ae57fde9.question: This figure gave a speech concerning oars being replaced by reins and dolphins by horses at Thera. Arete aided this figure by revealing Alcinous’s decision to relinquish this woman if she was found not to be a virgin. Acastus drove her out of Iolcus after she tricked Alcestis’s sisters into cutting up their father Pelias. Her children’s grave stood next to a wall at which another woman threw herself after this woman gave her a poisoned robe. This woman departed in a chariot pulled by dragons after killing Glauce and her own children, according to Euripides. For 10 points, name this sorceress from Colchis who poisoned the dragon guarding the Golden Fleece and married Jason.
